fr.recommendations: To promote effective emergency response, maintaining low-dense vegetation around the main building and landscaped areas could enhance visibility and accessibility for responders. Ensuring that the sidewalk network remains unobstructed and connected may facilitate easier navigation and movement for emergency personnel across the property. Additionally, considering the development of alternative access points in the rear, where access may currently be limited, could support more efficient responder deployment and staging. Such efforts might improve access to all building sides, enabling quicker and more effective emergency operations. These measures would promote smoother responder operations and improve overall emergency response effectiveness on the property.

wildfire.recommendations: Creating more separation between vegetation and the structures, including reducing the concentration of vegetation adjacent to both the main structure and the fence, could improve defensible space and reduce fuel continuity. Thinning and managing the tree cluster can decrease fuel concentration, helping to minimize potential pathways for fire spread. Enhancing open space around the property and ensuring routine maintenance of the access route can support firefighter access and improve suppression efforts. Addressing the vegetated areas adjacent to the access route by reducing vegetation density and increasing clear space can further facilitate safe and efficient ingress and egress, enhancing long-term wildfire resilience and readiness.
